claims.evidence_text: The South Dakota State University (SDSU) current recommendation is 2.5 lbs of N per bushel, which is reduced by accounting for the various credits
claims.claim_summary: SDSU N rate recommendation for wheat is 2.5 lb N per bushel of yield goal, before credits.

claims.evidence_text: N recommendation = 150 lbs N acre - 30 lbs NO3-N acre - 40 lbs acre - 0 lbs acre = 80 lbs N acre
claims.claim_summary: Example: 60 bu/a yield goal x 2.5 = 150 lb N/acre total required before credits (net 80 lb N/acre after soil N and legume credits).

claims.evidence_text: The first application can be made at planting, while the second should be applied prior to jointing or stem elongation (Feekes 4-5). It needs to be pointed out that N fertilizer should not be applied over snow.
claims.claim_summary: Split N for wheat: first at planting, second prior to jointing/stem elongation (Feekes 4-5); winter wheat needs low N in fall with rapid uptake in spring.

claims.evidence_text: N applied too early can increase lodging and excessive tillering, while N applied too late can result in lower yields... Late season N applications do not impact yield and may only increase protein.
claims.claim_summary: N applied too early increases lodging and excessive tillering; too late lowers yield; late-season N raises grain protein without yield gain.

claims.evidence_text: N applied too early can increase lodging and excessive tillering... some varieties are very susceptible to lodging
claims.claim_summary: Early/excessive N application increases wheat lodging; some varieties are highly lodging-susceptible.

claims.evidence_text: Late season N applications do not impact yield and may only increase protein... The market basis for high-protein wheat will also warrant consideration of in-season N applications.
claims.claim_summary: Late-season/in-season N applications increase wheat grain protein without affecting yield.

claims.evidence_text: Post-plant winter wheat Delay until soil temperature < 50 F. Avoid UAN, if leaching is possibility. Subsurface applications are more efficient.
claims.claim_summary: Fall post-plant winter wheat N should be delayed until soil temperature is below 50 F; subsurface placement is more efficient than surface.
